questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
The table below shows some information of elements Y and Z.
YZ
Atomic number3553
Number of occupied electron shells in the atoms45
Number of electrons in the outermost shell in the atoms77

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: State the electronic arrangement of an atom of Y.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: Draw the electron diagram for a molecule of Z, showing ELECTRONS IN THE OUTERMOST SHELLS only.
sub_questions.answer: [IMG:q1_img_0.png]

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text:
An experiment for Y and Z is performed as shown in the set-up below. Dilute hydrochloric acid is added to the  \( K_{2}SO_{3} \)  crystals, then the whole set-up is covered with a lid.

[IMG:q1_img_1.png]

children.label: i
children.question_text: \(K_2CO_3\) crystals react with dilute hydrochloric acid to give sulphur dioxide gas. Write a chemical equation for the reaction, showing all state symbols.

children.label: ii
children.question_text: State the expected observation in Container (1) and write an ionic equation for the reaction involved.
children.answer:
Reddish brown changes to colourless.
\(Br_2+SO_2+2H_2O \rightarrow\ 2Br^-+SO_4^{2-}+4H^+\)
OR \(Y_2+SO_2+2H_2O \rightarrow\ 2Y^-+SO_4^{2-}+4H^+\)

children.label: iii
children.question_text:
It is expected that the observation in Container (2) is similar to that in Container (1).
Suggest a reason for this expectation based on electronic arrangement.
children.answer: Y and Z has the same number of electrons in the outermost shells, hence they have similar chemical properties.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
An experiment was performed to deduce the empirical formula of an insoluble chloride of a metal M. At room temperature, different volumes of a  \( 0.50 \, mol \, dm^{-3} \, M(NO_{3})_{n}(aq) \)  were added to six beakers each containing  \( 50 \, cm^{3} \)  of  \( 0.36 \, mol \, dm^{-3} \, HCl(aq) \) . The  \( MCl_{n}(s) \)  obtained in each beaker was filtered, washed, dried and weighed. The mass of  \( MCl_{n}(s) \)  obtained and the corresponding volume of  \( M(NO_{3})_{n}(aq) \)  added were plotted on the graph below.

[IMG:q2_img_0.png]

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Suggest why the masses of  \( MCl_{n}(s) \)  for the last three points in the graph are the same.
sub_questions.answer: It is because for the last three points in the graph, the amount of  \( M(NO_{3})_{n} / M^{n+} \)  added is in excess.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.answer:
(i)

volume of M(NO_{3})_{n}(aq) = 18 cm^{3}

children.label: i
children.question_text:
By sketching on the graph above, deduce the volume of the  \( M(NO_{3})_{n}(aq) \)  that can completely react with  \( 50\ cm^{3} \)  of  \( 0.36\ mol\ dm^{-3}\ HCl(aq) \) .

Volume of \(M(NO_3)_n(aq)\) = ________ \(cm^3\)

children.label: ii
children.question_text: Hence, calculate the number of moles of  \( M(NO_{3})_{n}(aq) \)  that can completely react with the HCl(aq).

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: Determine, by calculation, the empirical formula of the chloride of M. Hence, deduce whether M would be silver or lead.
sub_questions.answer:
No. of moles of  \( Cl^{-} \) :  \( (50 / 1000) \times 0.36 = 0.018 \, mol \)
Ratio of metal ions to chloride ions = 0.009 : 0.018 = 1 : 2. The empirical formula of the metal chloride is  \( MCl_{2} \) .
M would be lead because the ratio of Pb to Cl in its empirical formula is 1:2 while the ratio of Ag to Cl is 1:1.

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Draw a three-dimensional diagram to represent the shape of each of the following molecules :

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text:
\( H_{3}NBH_{3} \)  has a structure similar to that of ethane. Its electron diagram is shown below (showing electrons in the outermost shells only).

[IMG:q3_img_0.png]

children.label: i
children.question_text: Which of the H–B, B–N and N–H bonds would be native covalent bond(s)? Explain your answer.
children.answer:
• B–N would be the dative covalent bond.
• The lone electron pair on nitrogen atom of  \( NH_{3} \)  is donated to form a dative covalent bond with the boron atom of  \( BH_{3} \) .

children.label: ii
children.question_text: Explain why \(H_3NBH_3\) is a solid but ethane is a gas at room conditions.
children.answer:
• Both are van der Waals' forces between their respective molecules.
• As \(H_3NBH_3\) is polar but ethane is not, the van der Waals' forces between \(H_3NBH_3\) molecules are stronger than those between ethane molecules.

children.label: iii
children.question_text: Under suitable conditions, \(H_3NBH_3\) can decompose into boron nitride and hydrogen. The structure of solid boron nitride is similar to that of graphite. Draw the structure of ONE LAYER of solid boron nitride (Note : B and N are in alternate positions).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Eggshells mainly contain calcium carbonate and a small amount of organic substances. The percentage by mass of calcium carbonate in a sample of eggshell was determined by the following steps :

Step (1): The sample was ground into powder.
Step (2): 0.204 g of the powder was put into a conical flask. After that, 25.00 cm \( ^{3} \)  of 0.200 M HCl(aq) and 5 cm \( ^{3} \)  of ethanol were added.
Step (3): The mixture was heated for 15 minutes.
Step (4): After cooling down, the mixture was titrated with 0.102 M NaOH(aq) using an indicator X.

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Explain why the sample was ground into powder in Step (1).
sub_questions.answer: To increase the surface area of eggshell for increasing the reaction rate.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: Suggest why ethanol was added in Step (2).
sub_questions.answer: To dissolve organic substances in eggshell.

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: Suggest why the mixture was heated for 15 minutes in Step (3).
sub_questions.answer: Speed up the reaction between the calcium carbonate in the sample with HCl(aq). / To make sure that the reaction is complete.

sub_questions.label: d
sub_questions.question_text: The mixture turned from colourless to pale pink at the end point of titration in Step (4). Name indicator X.
sub_questions.answer: phenolphthalein

sub_questions.label: e
sub_questions.question_text:
16.85 cm \( ^{3} \)  of NaOH(aq) was needed to reach the end point of titration in Step (4). Calculate the percentage by mass of calcium carbonate in the sample.
(Relative atomic masses : C = 12.0, O = 16.0, Ca = 40.1)
sub_questions.answer:
Number of moles of  \( CaCO_{3} \)  in the sample
\(  = (0.200 \times 25.00 - 0.102 \times 16.85) \times 10^{-3} \times \frac{1}{2}  \)
\(  = 1.64 \times 10^{-3}  \)
Percentage by mass of  \( CaCO_{3} \)  in the sample
\(  = 1.64 \times 10^{-3} \times 100.1 \div 0.204 \times 100\%  \)
\(  = 80.5\%  \)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: The molecular formula of an organic compound W is  \( C_{4}H_{6}O_{4} \) . It is soluble in water.

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: When a piece of magnesium ribbon is placed into an aqueous solution of W, hydrogen gas evolves. According to this observation, suggest a functional group that W may contain.
sub_questions.answer: Carboxyl group / -COOH group

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: It is known that one mole of W can completely react with two moles of NaOH.

children.label: i
children.question_text: Draw TWO possible structures of W.

children.label: ii
children.question_text:
Consider the following thermochemical equation of a neutralisation reaction in standard conditions :

\(  C_{4}H_{6}O_{4}(aq)+2NaOH(aq)\rightarrow Na_{2}C_{4}H_{4}O_{4}(aq)+2H_{2}O(l)\quad\Delta H^{\theta}=y\ kJ\ mol^{-1} \)

State the meaning of the term ‘standard enthalpy change of neutralisation’, and deduce the standard enthalpy change of neutralisation for this reaction in terms of y.
children.answer:
• The enthalpy change when solutions of an acid and an alkali / a base react together under standard conditions to produce 1 mole of water.
• As indicated in the equation, the reaction produces 2 moles of water, hence y / 2 represents the standard enthalpy change of neutralisation for that reaction.

children.label: iii
children.question_text: The standard enthalpy change of neutralisation between HCl(aq) and NaOH(aq) is -57.3 kJ mol \( ^{-1} \) . Explain whether the enthalpy change deduced in (ii) above should be more negative than, less negative than or equal to -57.3 kJ mol \( ^{-1} \) .
children.answer:
• Less negative than -57.3 kJ mol \( ^{-1} \) .
• W is a weak acid when compared with HCl(aq), energy is needed to ionise the hydrogen in the carboxyl group.

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
The diagrams below show the component of a chemical cell, an experimental set-up and how the pointer of the voltmeter deflects when the set-up is connected to the component.

[IMG:q6_img_0.png]

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Why does the pointer of the voltmeter deflect as shown when a few drops of water are added to the component?
sub_questions.answer:
• To provide an aqueous medium so as to produce mobile ions.
• Magnesium is higher than copper in the electrochemical series / ECS and release electrons. The electrons pass from the negative pole of the voltmeter to the positive pole, producing to a positive reading.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: Write the half equation for the change that occurs at each of the following electrodes when the pointer of the voltmeter deflects :

children.label: i
children.question_text: anode

children.label: ii
children.question_text: cathode

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text:
Consider the following design modified from Diagram (1) by only removing the copper coating at side A :

Draw on Diagram (2) the expected position of the pointer of the voltmeter when water is added to the component.
sub_questions.answer: The position of the pointer is higher than 0 and lower than the reading in Diagram (1).

sub_questions.label: d
sub_questions.question_text: In the design in part (c) above, a redox reaction occurs at side A when water is added to the component.

children.label: i
children.question_text: Write a chemical equation for the reaction.

children.label: ii
children.question_text: Name this type of reaction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
An experiment is performed to study the following reaction :

\( \mathrm{Ba(OH)_{2}\bullet8H_{2}O(s)+2NH_{4}Cl(s)\rightarrow BaCl_{2}(s)+10H_{2}O(l)+2NH_{3}(g)} \)

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: When the two solid reactants are mixed and stirred in a conical flask, ammonia gas with a characteristic pungent smell is formed. Explain how ammonia gas can be tested.
sub_questions.answer:
• Put a moist red litmus paper near the mouth of the conical flask. 
• Ammonia gas dissolves in water to give  \( OH^{-} \)  ions which turn red litmus paper to blue.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: \( \mathrm{Ba(OH)_2 \bullet 8H_2O(s)} \)  is an alkali. What is meant by the term ‘alkali’?
sub_questions.answer: Alkali is a water soluble substance reacting with an acid to give salt and water only.

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: The standard enthalpy change of formation of  \( \mathrm{Ba(OH)_2 \bullet 8H_2O(s)}\ is\ 3345 \, \mathrm{kJ \, mol^{-1}} \) .

children.label: i
children.question_text: Write a thermochemical equation for the standard enthalpy change of formation of  \( \mathrm{Ba(OH)_2 \bullet 8 H_2O(s)} \) .

children.label: ii
children.question_text:
Calculate the standard enthalpy change of the reaction between \( \mathrm{Ba(OH)_2 \bullet 8H_2O(s)} and \mathrm{NH_4Cl(s)} \) .
(Standard enthalpy changes of formation :
\( NH_{3}(g) = -46\ kJ\ mol^{-1}, H_{2}O(l) = -286\ kJ\ mol^{-1}, NH_{4}Cl(s) = -314\ kJ\ mol^{-1},\)
\(BaCl_{2}(s) = -859\ kJ\ mol^{-1} \) )

children.label: iii
children.question_text: Hence, explain whether the temperature of the mixture would increase, decrease or remain unchanged during the reaction.
children.answer: As the reaction is endothermic, the temperature of the mixture would decrease.

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds
questions.question_text: Describe how 1,2-dibromoethane can be produced from crude oil, via an alkene, using appropriate chemicals and processes. Write the chemical equations for the reactions involved.
questions.answer:
Chemical knowledge (1 mark for each point, a maximum of 5 marks)
• Separation of crude oil by oil refinery / fractional distillation gives heavy oil or fuel oil, etc.
• Cracking gives a mixture of small molecules with ethene.
• C+H_{16} \rightarrow CH_{2}=CH_{2} + C_{3}H_{12}
• Fractional distillation of the above mixture gives ethene.
• Addition reaction of ethene and bromine gives 1,2-dibromoethane.
• CH_{2}=CH_{2} + Br_{2} \rightarrow BrCH_{2}CH_{2}Br
Communication mark

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following reaction mixture at  \( 25^{\circ} \) C in a closed container of fixed volume :
\( \begin{array}{c}2\mathrm{NO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})\rightleftharpoons\mathrm{N}_{2}\mathrm{O}_{4}(\mathrm{~g})\\ \mathrm{brown}\quad\mathrm{colourless}\end{array} \)

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text:
With reference to the table below, calculate a. Hence, determine the equilibrium constant \( K_{c} \) for the reaction at \( 25^{\circ}C \) .
NO2(g)N2O4(g)
Concentration at start / mol dm−30.04000.0010
Concentration at equilibrium / mol dm−30.0323a

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: The temperature of the mixture is increased to  \( 55^{\circ} \) C and its colour eventually turns darker. Deduce whether the reaction above is endothermic or exothermic.
sub_questions.answer:
• More  \( NO_{2} \)  is formed and the equilibrium position shifts to the left / shifts to the reactant side when the temperature increases.
• Increasing temperature shifts the equilibrium position to endothermic direction. Therefore, the forward reaction is exothermic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
The structure of a compound Y is shown below :

\(\mathrm{H_{2}C=CHCH_{2}OH} \)

sub_questions.label: a

children.answer:
\( H_{2}C=CH-CH_{2}-Cl + NaOH \rightarrow H_{2}C=CH-CH_{2}-OH + NaCl\) / 
\(H_{2}C=CH-CH_{2}-Cl + OH^{-} \rightarrow H_{2}C=CH-CH_{2}-OH + Cl^{-} \)

children.label: ii
children.question_text: Name this type of reaction.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: On heating under reflux, a compound L reacts with KOH(aq) to give Y and  \( CH_{3}COO^{-}K^{+} \) .
sub_questions.answer:
(i)
\[ \mathrm{H_{2}C=CH-CH_{2}-O\longrightarrow C-CH_{3}} \]
(ii)

(c)

children.label: i
children.question_text: Suggest the structural formula of L.

children.label: ii
children.question_text: Draw a labelled diagram to show the set-up for this reaction.

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: Under suitable conditions, Y can form a polymer. Write the repeating unit of the polymer.
sub_questions.answer: [IMG:ans_q10_b_1.jpg]

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
The structures of some compounds are shown below:
CompoundStructure
W[IMG:q11_img_0.png]
X[IMG:q11_img_1.png]
Y[IMG:q11_img_2.png]
Z[IMG:q11_img_3.png]

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Which one of W, X, Y or Z is a tertiary alcohol?
sub_questions.answer: Z

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text:
Label all chiral centre(s), if any, by using ‘*’ on the structure of W below.

[IMG:q11_img_4.png]
sub_questions.answer: [IMG:ans_q11_a_0.jpg]

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text:
Heating X under reflux in 2 M NaOH(aq) can form an optically active organic compound U and an optically inactive organic compound V. Draw the respective structures of U and V.

U :

V :
sub_questions.answer:
U : HOCH₂C(CH₃)₂CH(OH)CO₂⁻Na⁺ / HOCH₂C(CH₃)₂CH(OH)CO₂Na 

V :  \( H_{2}NCH_{2}CH_{2}CO_{2}^{-}Na^{+} \) / \( H_{2}NCH_{2}CH_{2}CO_{2}Na \)

sub_questions.label: d
sub_questions.question_text:
Consider the following reagents :

\(Br_{2}(aq) \quad acidified\ K_{2}Cr_{2}O_{7}(aq) \quad Na_{2}CO_{3}(aq)\)

children.label: i
children.question_text: Suggest which one of the reagents can be used to perform a chemical test, in order to distinguish X from W, Y and Z.

children.label: ii
children.question_text: State the observation in the test involved in (i). Explain your answer.
children.answer:
• Colourless gas evolves when  \( Na_{2}CO_{3}(aq) \)  is put into X, but not W, Y nor Z.
• Only X has a carboxyl group but W, Y and Z do not.

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 12: Patterns in the Chemical World
questions.question_text:
An experiment was performed to study the following reaction :

\(\mathrm{KO_{2}CCH(OH)CH(OH)CO_{2}Na(aq)+3H_{2}O_{2}(aq)\rightarrow HCO_{2}K(aq)+HCO_{2}Na(aq)+2CO_{2}(g)+4H_{2}O(l)} \)
\(\quad \quad colourless\)

When  \( 10 \, cm^{3} \)  of  \( 0.25 \, M \, KO_{2}CCH(OH)CH(OH)CO_{2}Na(aq) \)  and  \( 3 \, cm^{3} \)  of  \( 6\% \, H_{2}O_{2}(aq) \)  were mixed at  \( 60^{\circ}C \) , it was found that only a few gas bubbles evolved. Then a small amount of pink  \( \mathrm{CoCl}_{2}(aq) \)  solution was added to the mixture. Gas bubbles formed vigorously and the mixture turned to green due to the formation of a cobalt(III) compound. When no more gas evolved, the green mixture turned back to pink.

There is a view saying that cobalt illustrates THREE characteristics of transition metals according to the observation of this experiment. Suggest reasons to support this view.
questions.answer:
• Cobalt / Co^{2+} acts as a catalyst: The rate of formation of gas bubbles (CO_{2}) increases / rate of reaction increases when Co^{2+} ions are added,
• and the pink  \( Co^{2+} \)  ions regenerate / remain chemically unchanged / do not consume at the end of reaction.
• Coloured ion / formation of coloured compound:  \( Co^{2+}(aq) \)  is pink / the cobalt(III) compound formed is green.
• Variable oxidation states: Cobalt has cobalt(II) and cobalt(III) compounds / can exist as  \( Co^{2+} \)  and  \( Co^{3+} \) .

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
With reference to the set-up shown below, describe how the effect of concentration of HCl(aq) on the rate of the reaction can be studied. Your answer should include TWO labelled curves sketched on the graph below, one using solid line and the other one using dotted line. Label all curves and axes.

[IMG:q13_img_0.png]

[IMG:q13_img_2.png]
questions.answer:
Chemical knowledge
Labelled graph

[IMG:q13_img_1.png]

(1 mark for each point from below, a maximum of 3 marks)
• Measure the volume of  \( H_{2}(g) \)  formed at different time intervals and plot a curve.
• The slope of the curve represents the rate of reaction.
• Repeat the experiment with different concentrations of HCl(aq).
• Fair comparison - other than concentration of HCl(aq), all other conditions should be the same. Communication mark
